frameworks.name: computational phenotyping
frameworks.one_line: Represent individuals as parameter vectors from models fit to behavior and neural data.

frameworks.name: mechanistic cognitive modeling
frameworks.one_line: Model parameters carry interpretation in terms of cognitive processes.

core_question: How can parameters from computational models fit to individual data characterize differences in personality, development, and psychopathology?
key_assumptions: model parameters have meaningful cognitive-process interpretations
individuals are continuous points in a parameter space
computational phenotypes complement rather than replace trait and symptom measures
key_findings: computational phenotyping has yielded new insights into individual differences
approach has been applied to developmental trajectories
approach has been applied to psychopathology
interpretations: mechanistic parameter-space representation supports quantitative predictions of future behavior and brain activity
computational phenotypes complement traditional trait and symptom measures
limitations_noted: challenges remain that the authors survey at the end
